papers.title_en: Scaling Point-in-Time Language Models
papers.title_zh: 扩展即时语言模型
papers.authors: Bryan T. Kelly, Semyon Malamud, Johannes Schwab, Teng Andrea Xu
papers.abstract_en: Large language models trained on unrestricted internet corpora inevitably embed information from the future, introducing lookahead bias that compromises the validity of backtests and causal inference in finance and the social sciences. Point-in-time language modelsâtrained exclusively on text available up to each calendar dateâeliminate this leakage by construction, but existing efforts typically produce models that lag substantially behind their unconstrained counterparts. We show that this performance gap can be narrowed through scale. Training decoder-only transformers with up to 4 billion parameters on 1 trillion chronologically filtered tokens from FineWeb, we construct a sequence of monthly model checkpoints spanning 2013â2024. Across a range of common-sense reasoning and language understanding benchmarks, our models approach the performance of leading open-weight models of comparable size (such as Gemma-3-4B and LLaMA-7B) trained on temporally unrestricted data, although a performance gap remains on several tasks. Finally, in a strict out-of-sample economic evaluation task, portfolios built from point-in-time embeddings achieve robust positive Sharpe ratios and perform close to full-sample counterparts that violate temporal validity, indicating that chronologically consistent language models can extract economically meaningful signals without relying on look-ahead bias. We release the complete pipelineâincluding dataset construction, training infrastructure, and evaluation codeâto enable reproducible point-in-time language modeling and to support research applications that require strict temporal validity.
papers.abstract_zh: 在无限制的互联网语料库上训练的大型语言模型不可避免地嵌入了来自未来的信息，引入了前瞻性偏差，这损害了金融和社会科学研究中回测和因果推断的有效性。点时间语言模型——仅在每个日历日期之前可用的文本上训练——通过构造消除了这种泄露，但现有的努力通常产生的模型远远落后于它们的无限制对应物。我们展示了通过规模可以缩小这种性能差距。我们在FineWeb上训练了高达40亿参数的仅解码器变换器，使用了1万亿个按时间顺序过滤的标记，构建了一系列从2013年到2024年的月度模型检查点。在一系列常识推理和语言理解基准测试中，我们的模型接近于在时间上无限制数据上训练的同类大小的领先开放权重模型（如Gemma-3-4B和LLaMA-7B）的性能，尽管在几项任务上仍存在性能差距。最后，在一项严格的样本外经济评估任务中，基于点时间嵌入构建的投资组合实现了稳健的正夏普比率，并接近于违反时间有效性的全样本对应物，表明按时间顺序一致的语言模型可以提取经济上有意义的信号，而不需要依赖前瞻性偏差。我们发布了完整的流程——包括数据集构建、训练基础设施和评估代码——以支持可复制的点时间语言建模，并支持需要严格时间有效性的研究应用。

papers.title_en: Preference for Explainable AI
papers.title_zh: 偏好可解释的人工智能
papers.authors: Alex Chan
papers.abstract_en: Participants acted as loan officers deciding whether to approve real $10,000-loans issued by a private U.S. lender using an AIâs default-risk predictions. When explanations revealed that the AI penalized non-White or female borrowers, participants were more likely to override the AIâs profit-maximizing recommendation. When their bonuses depended on repayment, however, they sought predictions but avoided explanations, consistent with willful ignorance; this effect faded when explanations were framed as purely financial or demographics were hidden. A secondary experiment reveals a novel bias: participants failed to reason contingently and undervalued explanations even when these complemented private information and improved decision accuracy.
papers.abstract_zh: 参与者扮演贷款官员，决定是否批准一家美国私人贷款机构根据人工智能的违约风险预测发放的1万美元贷款。当解释显示人工智能对非白人或女性借款人进行惩罚时，参与者更有可能推翻人工智能的利润最大化建议。然而，当他们的奖金取决于还款时，他们寻求预测但避免解释，这与故意无知一致；当解释被框定为纯粹的财务或隐藏人口统计数据时，这种效应会减弱。一个次要实验揭示了一种新的偏见：参与者未能进行条件推理，即使这些解释补充了私人信息并提高了决策准确性，他们也低估了解释的价值。

papers.title_en: The (Lack of) Efficacy of Social Belonging and Growth Mindset Interventions Among College Students
papers.title_zh: 大学生中社会归属感和成长型思维干预的（缺乏）效果
papers.authors: Abid N. Alam, Philip Oreopoulos, Uros Petronijevic
papers.abstract_en: Using four large-scale experiments across two major Canadian universities, we experimentally evaluate the effects of growth mindset and social belonging interventions on student outcomes. In a sample of nearly 12,000 students, we find no immediate or dynamic effects on student grades and no effect on persistence through university. We further combine survey and administrative data with machine learning methods to explore treatment effect heterogeneity, finding no evidence of meaningful variation in treatment effects across student subgroups. Despite the recent promise of these light-touch interventions, our findings indicate further research is required to identify the contexts in which their benefits generalize.
papers.abstract_zh: 通过在加拿大两所主要大学进行的四项大规模实验，我们实验性地评估了成长心态和社会归属感干预对学生结果的影响。在近12,000名学生的样本中，我们发现这些干预对学生成绩没有立即或动态的影响，也没有影响学生在大学的持续学习。我们进一步结合调查和行政数据以及机器学习方法来探索治疗效果的异质性，发现没有证据表明不同学生子群体间治疗效果有显著变化。尽管这些轻触式干预最近显示出希望，但我们的发现表明需要进一步研究以确定其益处普遍适用的情境。

papers.title_en: The Limits of Targeted Hiring Subsidies: Evidence from the Work Opportunity Tax Credit
papers.title_zh: 有针对性招聘补贴的局限性：来自工作机会税收抵免的证据
papers.authors: Manisha Jain, Corina Mommaerts, Jeffrey Weaver
papers.abstract_en: Employer-side wage subsidies are widely used to promote employment among disadvantaged workers. We study how such subsidies translate into firm hiring behavior using the federal Work Opportunity Tax Credit, which subsidizes up to 40% of first-year wages and covers over two million hires annually. Using linked administrative data from Wisconsin and multiple quasi-experimental designs, we find consistent and precise null effects on hiring, earnings, retention, and related outcomes across designs and firm types. Original data on firm hiring practices suggest two mechanisms that can limit employer-side subsidy efficacy: perceived legal risks discourage eligibility screening and organizational frictions attenuate decision-makersâ responsiveness.
papers.abstract_zh: 雇主方工资补贴被广泛用于促进弱势工人的就业。我们通过联邦“工作机会税收抵免”政策来研究这类补贴如何转化为公司的招聘行为，该政策补贴高达40%的第一年工资，并每年覆盖超过两百万的雇佣。利用威斯康星州的链接行政数据和多种准实验设计，我们发现在不同设计和公司类型中，对招聘、收入、留职及相关结果的影响始终是精确且为零。关于公司招聘实践的原始数据表明，有两个机制可能限制雇主方补贴的有效性：感知的法律风险阻碍了资格筛选，而组织摩擦减弱了决策者的响应性。

papers.title_en: The Optimal Use of AI in Financial Regulation
papers.title_zh: 人工智能在金融监管中的最优应用
papers.authors: Christopher Clayton, Antonio Coppola
papers.abstract_en: We study whether AI methods applied to large-scale portfolio holdings data can improve macroprudential financial regulation. We build a graph-based deep learning model tailored to security-level data on the holdings of financial intermediaries. The architecture incorporates economic priors and learns latent representations of both assets and investors from the network structure of portfolio positions. Applied to the universe of non-bank financial intermediaries, covering nearly $40 trillion in wealth, the model substantially outperforms existing approaches in out-of-sample forecasts of intermediary trading behavior, including in crisis episodes. The model has more than ten times the explanatory power for the cross-sectional variation in asset returns during stress events compared to traditional approaches, and it outperforms existing systemic risk metrics at the institution level. Its learned representations show that the holdings network encodes rich, economically interpretable information about fire- sale vulnerability. The architecture is fully inductive, producing informative estimates even when entire asset classes or investors are withheld from training. We embed our empirical approach into a macroprudential optimal policy framework to formalize why these objects matter for policy and welfare. We show that even in an equilibrium environment subject to the Lucas critique, the predictive information from the model improves welfare by sharpening the cross-sectional targeting of policy interventions, and we demonstrate a complementarity between prediction and structural knowledge.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们研究了应用于大规模投资组合持有数据的人工智能方法是否能改善宏观审慎金融监管。我们构建了一个基于图的深度学习模型，专门针对金融中介机构持有的证券级数据。该架构结合了经济先验，并从投资组合头寸的网络结构中学习资产和投资者的潜在表示。应用于涵盖近40万亿美元财富的非银行金融中介机构，该模型在样本外预测中介交易行为方面显著优于现有方法，包括在危机时期。与传统方法相比，该模型对压力事件期间资产回报的横截面变化的解释能力超过十倍，并且在机构层面的表现优于现有的系统性风险指标。其学习到的表示显示，持有网络编码了关于火灾销售脆弱性的丰富、经济上可解释的信息。该架构是完全归纳的，即使在训练中完全排除了整个资产类别或投资者，也能产生有信息量的估计。我们将实证方法嵌入到宏观审慎最优政策框架中，以正式化这些对象对政策和福利的重要性。我们展示了即使在受到卢卡斯批评的均衡环境中，模型的预测信息也能通过细化政策干预的横截面定位来改善福利，并且我们展示了预测和结构知识之间的互补性。

papers.title_en: California Billionaires: Wealth, Taxes, and Wealth Tax Revenue Estimates
papers.title_zh: 加利福尼亚亿万富翁：财富、税收与财富税收入估计
papers.authors: Jasper Boll, Emmanuel Saez, Gabriel Zucman
papers.abstract_en: This paper documents the wealth of Californiaâs billionaires and the taxes they pay. California billionairesâ wealth exceeds $2 trillion today, the equivalent of 50% of Californiaâs GDP. It has grown 144% from 2023 to 2025, fueled by the AI boom. Over the longer run, the real wealth of Californiaâs billionaire classâthe 0.0002% richest householdsâhas been multiplied by 30 from 1982 to 2025, while average real family income in California has about doubled. California billionaires pay about 0.2% of their wealth in California income tax ($3.2 billion/year), representing 2.4% of total California income tax revenue on average over 2023-2025. Using Securities and Exchange Commission data from Alphabet, Meta, Oracle, and Nvidia since 2004, we estimate the trajectory of wealth, income, and taxes paid by the top 4 California billionairesâPage, Brin, Zuckerberg, Ellison (through 2020), and Huang (since 2021)âfocusing on their business wealth. This group alone holds nearly $1 trillion in business wealth, almost half of total California billionaire wealth. For this group, wealth growth (+322% over 2023-2025) and low taxation (0.04% of wealth in annual California income tax) are more pronounced. The proposed one-off California billionaire tax of 5%, payable over 5 years, is both small relative to California billionairesâ wealth gains and large relative to the taxes they currently pay. We estimate that it could raise about $100 billion, with comparatively minor impacts on income tax revenue. Using empirical estimates of mobility responses to wealth taxation, we find that an annual wealth tax on California billionaires could raise substantial additional revenue even after accounting for income tax losses due to mobility.
papers.abstract_zh: 本文记录了加州亿万富翁的财富及其所缴纳的税款。目前，加州亿万富翁的财富超过2万亿美元，相当于加州GDP的50%。从2023年到2025年，这一数字增长了144%，由人工智能（AI）的繁荣推动。从更长期来看，从1982年到2025年，加州亿万富翁阶层——最富有的0.0002%家庭——的实际财富增长了30倍，而加州家庭的平均实际收入大约翻了一番。加州亿万富翁大约支付其财富的0.2%作为加州所得税（每年32亿美元），这代表了2023-2025年加州所得税总收入的2.4%。利用自2004年以来美国证券交易委员会的数据，我们估算了加州四大亿万富翁——佩奇、布林、扎克伯格、埃里森（截至2020年）和黄（自2021年以来）——的财富、收入和所缴税款的轨迹，重点关注他们的商业财富。仅这一群体就持有近1万亿美元的商业财富，几乎占加州亿万富翁总财富的一半。对于这一群体来说，财富增长（2023-2025年增长了322%）和低税收（每年加州所得税占财富的0.04%）更为明显。提议的一次性加州亿万富翁税为5%，可在5年内支付，相对于加州亿万富翁的财富增长来说很小，但相对于他们目前所支付的税款来说很大。我们估计这可以筹集约1000亿美元，对所得税收入的影响相对较小。利用对财富税流动性反应的实证估计，我们发现即使在考虑因流动性导致的所得税损失后，对加州亿万富翁征收年度财富税也能筹集大量额外收入。

papers.title_en: Divergence in Climate Change Communication: LLM-Based Evidence from the IPCC and the Press
papers.title_zh: 气候变化传播的分歧：基于LLM的IPCC与媒体证据
papers.authors: Sebastian Galiani, Franco Mettola La Giglia, Raul A. Sosa
papers.abstract_en: Public summaries of IPCC climate assessments lean toward the more severe end of the technical evidence. The pattern appears at two stages: the IPCCâs lead authors and member governments produce the Summary for Policymakers (SPM) from the Technical Summary (TS), and newspapers then cover the SPM. We use LLMs to score about 114,000 matched claim pairs from all six Assessment Reports (1990 to 2023) and ten major US and UK outlets. Both stages systematically shift toward the more severe end of the source while staying inside the IPCCâs accepted scientific ranges. The shift comes mainly from emphasizing higher-impact magnitudes within reported ranges, less from uncertainty compression, and almost none from selecting worst-case emissions scenarios. Left- and right-leaning outlets show similar patterns.
papers.abstract_zh: 政府间气候变化专门委员会（IPCC）气候评估的公众总结倾向于技术证据中更为严重的一端。这种模式出现在两个阶段：IPCC的主要作者和成员国从技术总结（TS）中制作政策制定者摘要（SPM），然后报纸报道SPM。我们使用大型语言模型（LLMs）对来自所有六次评估报告（1990年至2023年）和十个主要美国和英国媒体的约114,000对匹配声明进行评分。两个阶段都系统地向源头更严重的一端倾斜，同时保持在IPCC接受的科学范围内。这种转变主要来自于强调报告范围内更高影响幅度，较少来自于不确定性压缩，几乎没有来自于选择最坏情况的排放情景。左倾和右倾媒体显示出类似的模式。

papers.title_en: Old Space, New Space: A Commercial Revolution in Innovation?
papers.title_zh: 旧空间，新空间：商业创新的革命？
papers.authors: Ruben Gaetani, Alexander T. Whalley
papers.abstract_en: The emergence of firms like SpaceX and Blue Origin has made space a leading example of how private enterprise drives innovation, marking what many see as a sharp break between Old Space and New Space. Yet little systematic evidence documents when the transition to this new phase of space innovation occurred and which firms drove it. We use patent data to provide this measurement and find that the largest surge in space innovation occurred in the 1990s, coinciding with demand-side market creation, and preceding the entry of high-profile startups after 2005. Throughout this period and since, incumbent aerospace firms account for most of the space-related patenting, with entrants contributing a growing but minority share. The same geographic regions that dominated space innovation during the post-Apollo era remain dominant today. These patterns are consistent with directed technical change: incumbents direct R&D toward policy-created markets accessible from existing capabilities, while entrants bring science-based insights into domains requiring new paradigms. Our findings suggest that New Space is more closely connected to Old Space than prevailing narratives imply, and that government's most consequential role in space innovation may lie in constructing appropriable markets. We make patent data on space-related technologies available for future research.
papers.abstract_zh: 摘要： 像SpaceX和Blue Origin这样的公司的出现，使太空成为私营企业推动创新的典范，标志着许多人认为的旧太空与新太空之间的明显断裂。然而，很少有系统性的证据记录了太空创新过渡到这一新阶段的时间，以及哪些公司推动了这一过渡。我们使用专利数据来提供这种测量，并发现太空创新的最大激增发生在1990年代，与需求侧市场创造相吻合，并在2005年后高调初创公司的进入之前。在整个这一时期以及之后，现有的航空航天公司占据了大部分与太空相关的专利，而新进入者贡献了日益增长但仍然是少数的份额。在阿波罗时代之后主导太空创新的相同地理区域今天仍然占据主导地位。这些模式与有方向的技术变革一致：现有公司将研发引向政策创造的市场，这些市场可以从现有能力中获得，而新进入者将基于科学的洞察带入需要新范式的领域。我们的发现表明，新太空与旧太空的联系比流行的叙述所暗示的更为紧密，政府在太空创新中最具决定性的作用可能在于构建可占有的市场。我们为未来的研究提供与太空技术相关的专利数据。

papers.title_en: Endogenous Task Bundling, Skills and Automation
papers.title_zh: 内生任务捆绑、技能与自动化
papers.authors: Joshua S. Gans
papers.abstract_en: Empirical measures of AI's wage effect typically hold fixed the bundle of activities a worker is paid for at its pre-AI shape. We argue that this assumption hides much of the action. When automation breaks a job apart, firms decide how to recombine the surviving activities; whether they rebundle them into one broad role or split them into specialist roles changes which surviving skills the labour market actually rewards. A skill that played no role in the pre-AI wage can become the dominant component of the post-AI wage, while a skill that anchored the pre-AI wage can disappear from the schedule. We develop an assignment model in which the priced human bundle is endogenous, and we use it to show that a fixed-bundle wage regression can mis-sign the effect of AI exposure. In general, the omitted-redesign bias has no unconditional sign: it is the residual covariance between exposure and role-specific redesign terms. Under explicit sufficient conditions, exposure-correlated unbundling loads specialist comparative-advantage premia onto the exposure coefficient, while exposure-correlated rebundling loads a different, often opposite, omitted term. The sign must therefore be measured from local post-AI partition changes rather than assumed from exposure alone.
papers.abstract_zh: 实证研究中对人工智能工资效应的度量通常假设工人被支付的活动组合在人工智能之前是固定的。我们认为这个假设掩盖了许多动态变化。当自动化将工作分解时，企业决定如何重新组合剩余的活动；它们是将这些活动重新组合成一个广泛的职位，还是将其拆分为专业职位，这将改变劳动市场实际奖励哪些存活下来的技能。一个在人工智能之前对工资没有影响的技能可能成为人工智能之后工资的主要组成部分，而一个锚定了人工智能之前工资的技能可能从工资表中消失。我们开发了一个定价人类活动组合是内生的分配模型，并用它来展示固定组合工资回归可能会错误地指示人工智能暴露的效应。一般来说，遗漏的重新设计偏差没有无条件的符号：它是暴露和角色特定重新设计项之间的残差协方差。在明确的充分条件下，与暴露相关的解绑将专业比较优势溢价加载到暴露系数上，而与暴露相关的重新捆绑则加载一个不同的、通常是相反的遗漏项。因此，符号必须从当地人工智能后部分变化中测量，而不是仅从暴露中假设。

papers.title_en: Medical Innovation and Racial Health Disparities: Evidence from a Breakthrough Treatment
papers.title_zh: 医疗创新与种族健康差异：突破性治疗的证据
papers.authors: D. Mark Anderson, Kerwin Kofi Charles, Daniel I. Rees
papers.abstract_en: Whether medical innovation exacerbates or reduces racial health disparities remains an open question. We study surfactant replacement therapy (SRT), a life-saving intervention for premature infants with respiratory disorders. Before its approval by the FDA in 1989, premature Black infants were much less likely than their White counterparts to die from respiratory-related causes. Within a few years of FDA approval, the Black-White gap in respiratory-related neonatal mortality had essentially disappeared. Using 1980-2000 vital statistics data and non-respiratory-related mortality as a counterfactual outcome, we find that both Blacks and Whites benefited from the introduction of SRT, but White neonates experienced larger and more immediate reductions in mortality. We estimate that, by 1993, SRT had reduced respiratory-related mortality among White neonates by 46 percent, compared to 30 percent for Black neonates. These results are not explained by differences in health care access, as proxied by socioeconomic status or distance to the nearest neonatal intensive care unit. We conclude that racial differences in fetal pulmonary maturation, rather than barriers to access, likely drove the uneven impact of SRT on neonatal mortality.
papers.abstract_zh: 医学创新是否加剧或减少种族健康差异仍是一个未解之谜。我们研究了肺表面活性剂替代疗法（SRT），这是一种针对患有呼吸障碍的早产儿的救命干预措施。在1989年获得FDA批准之前，早产黑人婴儿比白人婴儿更不可能因呼吸相关原因死亡。在FDA批准后的几年内，黑人和白人婴儿在呼吸相关新生儿死亡率方面的差距基本消失。利用1980-2000年的人口统计数据和非呼吸相关死亡率作为反事实结果，我们发现黑人和白人婴儿都从SRT的引入中受益，但白人新生儿的死亡率降低更大且更迅速。我们估计，到1993年，SRT使白人新生儿的呼吸相关死亡率降低了46%，而黑人新生儿降低了30%。这些结果不能用医疗保健获取差异来解释，如通过社会经济地位或距离最近的新生儿重症监护室的距离来代表。我们得出结论，胎儿肺部成熟度的种族差异，而不是获取障碍，可能是SRT对新生儿死亡率不均衡影响的驱动因素。

papers.title_en: Estimating the Present Value of R&D Tax Benefits in the United States
papers.title_zh: 估算美国研发税收优惠的现值
papers.authors: Brandon Pecoraro, Nicholas C. Hoffman, Martin Lopez-Daneri, Elena C. Derby, Rachel Moore, Shannon E. Sledz
papers.abstract_en: Using a panel of confidential corporate tax returns, we provide the first direct estimates of the realized present value of corporate tax benefits from R&D credits and deductions in the United States. Realized tax benefits can deviate from statutory tax benefits because firms in loss status are typically unable to fully utilize credits and deductions to offset current-year taxes and instead must carry these attributes forward. We develop a novel procedure to track the intertemporal firm-level utilization of tax attributes generated by corporate R&D spending, and find that the present value of R&D tax benefits varies substantially with firmsâ loss status, age, and size. Old and large firms typically use R&D tax benefits quickly, while young firms â especially those that are small â frequently operate in loss status and use tax attributes more slowly. From 2012â2016, the average firm generated $0.41 in statutory tax benefits per dollar of R&D investment, with a realized present value of $0.36. Young and small firms in a loss position realized only $0.23 per dollar, a 44% decrease relative to the statutory benchmark.
papers.abstract_zh: 利用一系列保密的企业税务申报数据，我们首次直接估算了美国企业从研发税收抵免和扣除中获得的实际现值税收利益。实际税收利益可能与法定税收利益不同，因为亏损状态的公司通常无法充分利用抵免和扣除来抵消当年税收，而必须将这些属性结转。我们开发了一种新的方法来追踪企业研发支出产生的税收属性的跨时期利用情况，并发现研发税收利益的现值与公司的亏损状态、年龄和规模有显著差异。年长和大型公司通常迅速使用研发税收利益，而年轻公司——尤其是小型公司——经常处于亏损状态，更慢地使用税收属性。从2012年到2016年，平均每家企业每投入1美元研发投资，就产生了0.41美元的法定税收利益，实际现值为0.36美元。处于亏损状态的年轻和小型公司每投入1美元，实际现值仅为0.23美元，比法定基准减少了44%。

papers.title_en: Fiscal Policy and Transition Risk
papers.title_zh: 财政政策与转型风险
papers.authors: Stefano Carattini, Garth Heutel, Givi Melkadze, InÃ¨s Mourelon
papers.abstract_en: We study how climate policy can interact with distortionary fiscal policy and potentially lead to transition risk. Using an environmental dynamic stochastic general equilibrium model that features financial frictions and preexisting labor and capital taxes, we simulate a carbon tax and an abatement subsidy under different scenarios for returning carbon tax revenue or financing the subsidy. We find novel policy implications and important differences between the carbon tax and the subsidy. Under both policies, transition dynamics can differ sharply from long-run outcomes. For the carbon tax, transition dynamics depend on both financial frictions and the choice of revenue recycling. For the abatement subsidy, distortionary financing can generate contractionary transition dynamics, because of financial frictions. Macroprudential policy can mitigate transition risk under the carbon tax but has little effect under the subsidy.
papers.abstract_zh: 我们研究了气候政策如何与扭曲性财政政策相互作用，可能导致转型风险。我们使用一个包含金融摩擦和现有劳动与资本税的环境动态随机一般均衡模型，模拟了在不同情景下返还碳税收入或为补贴融资的碳税和减排补贴。我们发现了碳税和补贴之间的新颖政策含义和重要差异。在两种政策下，转型动态可能与长期结果截然不同。对于碳税，转型动态取决于金融摩擦和收入再循环的选择。对于减排补贴，扭曲性融资可能因金融摩擦而产生收缩性转型动态。宏观审慎政策可以在碳税下减轻转型风险，但在补贴下效果甚微。
